scope: Direct and adjacent competitors to Passage Caskets — a direct-to-consumer transparent CASKET-ONLY brand selling Serene S$499 / Tranquil S$999 / Eternal S$1,299 all-in (casket + island-wide delivery + venue positioning + GST). Pricing repositioned 2026-04-29: Serene S$800→S$499 (lead-gen / compassionate-social), Tranquil S$1,000→S$999, Eternal S$1,200→S$1,299. Set excludes digital bereavement platforms, livestream vendors, post-funeral admin services, CPF claim assistants, grief-counselling SaaS, will-writing platforms, and memorial-page SaaS — none of which compete with caskets.
methodology: Public websites + SG/MY industry chatter + Straits Times / CNA 'cost of dying' articles 2023-2025 + MOH death-cert data (~24,000 SG deaths/yr, ~65% wake+embalmed, ~35% direct cremation). SG traditional parlours do not publish casket-only prices, so casket markup ranges are triangulated mid-points from customer Google reviews and journalism — flagged 'hidden_in_package'. US DTC casket prices are public. JB/MY wholesale manufacturer prices are trade-only and inferred; flagged 'wholesale_only'.

competitors.name: Singapore Casket Company
competitors.target_market: SG Chinese middle-to-upper-middle families seeking 3-5 day traditional Buddhist/Taoist wake at the Lavender Street parlour
strengths: Owns Lavender Street flagship parlour (premium wake venue)
Full vertical from casket through cremation handling
weaknesses: Casket cost completely opaque — no published casket-only line item
Quote-on-call gating that under-50 SG buyers find friction-heavy
Brochure-ware website with no e-commerce or transparent pricing

competitors.name: Ang Chin Moh Funeral Directors
competitors.target_market: SG Chinese families paying mid-premium for a 'dignified' funeral with a recognised funeral director
strengths: 5th-generation family brand with strong founder presence (Ang Ziqian)
Differentiates on bereavement counselling and aftercare
Design-forward collateral compared to peer parlours
weaknesses: No public casket pricing — blocks comparison

competitors.name: Trinity Casket
competitors.target_market: SG Christian and Catholic families wanting full-service wake bundled with rites
weaknesses: No timber sourcing or sustainability disclosure

competitors.name: Nam Shan Casket
competitors.target_market: SG Chinese Buddhist / Taoist families for traditional wake services
strengths: 75+ year heritage among Chinese clan networks
Established hearse and paper-offering supply chain
weaknesses: No mobile-first ordering, phone-first quote model

competitors.name: Casket Fairprice
competitors.target_market: SG value-seeking families wanting straightforward funeral packages
strengths: Brand cue suggests transparent / value pricing
weaknesses: Casket itself never priced as a standalone SKU

competitors.name: Hock Hin Casket
competitors.target_market: SG Chinese families wanting traditional wake with mid-range pricing

competitors.name: Singapore Funeral Services
competitors.target_market: SG mid-market families across multiple faiths

competitors.name: Direct Funeral Services
competitors.target_market: SG value-seeking families of all faiths wanting transparent-priced funeral with no luxury markup
strengths: All-in packages listed online by faith
Strong SEO for funeral price queries
weaknesses: Still bundles casket — no casket-only SKU at S$499-1,200
Buyer must take a full package even if they only want a casket

competitors.name: 1Last
competitors.target_market: SG digital-native families wanting modern, design-led funeral package
strengths: Best-in-class design and brand voice in SG funeral category
weaknesses: Higher floor price than Passage casket-only
No sustainability or charity governance stack

competitors.name: Embrace Life
competitors.target_market: SG families wanting compassionate, low-pressure funeral planning
weaknesses: Limited transparency on casket cost share

competitors.name: Serenity Casket
competitors.target_market: SG Chinese Buddhist/Taoist families wanting value full-service wake with visible price anchors
strengths: Published starting prices for selected packages

competitors.name: Titan Casket
competitors.target_market: US bereaved families wanting transparent DTC casket priced under what funeral homes mark up
strengths: Closest structural analogue to Passage globally
Educational content on casket markup tactics
weaknesses: US-only, does not deliver to SG
No 3-hour delivery promise (US ships in days)
No charity / sustainability stack like Passage

competitors.name: Costco Caskets
competitors.target_market: US Costco members wanting low-cost casket without going through funeral home markup
strengths: Massive trust halo from Costco brand
Proves big-box DTC casket is normal

competitors.name: Walmart Caskets
competitors.target_market: US lowest-cost casket buyers shopping outside funeral homes
weaknesses: Generic listing UX, no grief-sensitive design

competitors.name: Millennium Casket Industries
competitors.target_market: SG and MY funeral parlours buying caskets at trade prices for resale into packages
competitors.estimated_casket_markup_sgd: Trade S$400 - S$900 (then resold by parlours at S$2-4K)
strengths: Trade-pricing baseline used by SG parlour markup
weaknesses: Not consumer-facing — SG families cannot buy direct
No DTC channel or transparent retail pricing

competitors.name: TSTee Wood Industry
competitors.target_market: SG/MY parlour wholesale buyers for hardwood caskets

competitors.name: Sinseeseng Casket
competitors.target_market: SG parlour partners and direct family buyers willing to phone in

competitors.name: Roger & Sons
competitors.target_market: Custom timber craftwork buyers; bespoke casket builds on commission
strengths: Could partner with Passage on premium SKU
weaknesses: Bespoke price floor too high for transparent DTC

competitors.name: White Artz Carpentry
competitors.target_market: SG joinery and parlour customers, occasional direct casket commissions

competitors.name: Soon Teck WoodWorks
competitors.target_market: SG trade buyers for wood goods and occasional casket fabrication

competitors.name: Zhen Feng Object Workshop
competitors.target_market: SG parlour partners and one-off custom buyers

competitors.name: The Eco Casket
competitors.target_market: Eco-conscious MY/SG families wanting biodegradable Finoboard casket alternative
weaknesses: Material is paper-based — not all SG buyers accept
Cross-border shipping into SG adds friction

competitors.name: Bamboo Eco Coffin
competitors.target_market: Eco-buyers across SE Asia wanting woven bamboo coffin
weaknesses: Freight & duty into SG is non-trivial
Aesthetic is niche, not mainstream SG taste

competitors.name: Ecoffins UK
competitors.target_market: UK / EU eco-funeral buyers; reference brand for sustainable casket category
weaknesses: Not a SG market threat directly

competitors.name: 1Bereavement (Direct Cremation SG)
competitors.target_market: SG families opting for direct cremation with no wake and minimum-spec casket
competitors.estimated_casket_markup_sgd: S$300 - S$600 cremation-spec casket bundled inside ~S$1.6-2.2K package
strengths: Lowest-cost route — bypasses wake and casket altogether
Aligned with the 35% direct-cremation share of SG deaths
weaknesses: No premium / dignity option — minimum-spec only
No FSC sourcing or charity stack

competitors.name: Simplicity Funerals SG
competitors.target_market: SG families wanting cremation-only with minimal ceremony
competitors.estimated_casket_markup_sgd: S$250 - S$550 cremation-spec inside S$1.2-1.8K package

competitors.name: Pure Cremation (UK reference)
competitors.target_market: UK families wanting unattended direct cremation
weaknesses: Direct-cremation-only — different category to casket DTC

competitors.name: DIY / Family-arranged direct burial
competitors.target_market: SG families with capacity to handle paperwork and casket sourcing themselves
competitors.estimated_casket_markup_sgd: S$400 - S$900 if family negotiates direct with manufacturer
weaknesses: Requires capacity and time bereaved families rarely have
Steep learning curve on permits and ROOM slots
No guidance on rites or logistics

competitors.name: Community / Welfare-org-arranged funerals
competitors.target_market: SG ComCare recipients, deceased without next-of-kin, social-worker referrals
competitors.estimated_casket_markup_sgd: Subsidised or free via welfare partner
strengths: Passage's free-casket programme is the structured answer here

categories.sg_traditional_parlour: Incumbent SG funeral parlours that bundle the casket inside an opaque all-in package; structurally hide casket markup of S$2,000-S$4,000.
categories.sg_transparent_funeral: SG funeral providers with transparent published package pricing — but still package-only, not casket-only DTC.
categories.dtc_casket_retailer: Direct-to-consumer casket-only retailers, primarily US (Titan, Costco, Walmart). Closest structural analogue to Passage globally.
categories.wholesale_manufacturer: Casket manufacturers (mostly SG/JB) who sell to parlour trade; not consumer-facing today, but represent both supply chain and bypass risk.
categories.eco_paper_casket: Eco-material casket alternatives (Finoboard, bamboo, willow, FSC). Niche but growing.
categories.direct_cremation_bypass: No-wake direct-cremation operators that minimise the casket spend by skipping the casket category entirely.
categories.diy_alt: DIY / family-arranged or community / welfare-coordinated funerals where no commercial casket is purchased.

landscape.marketSize: SG funeral services market estimated at S$300-450M annually; casket revenue share roughly S$60-100M (assuming S$2-4K hidden casket markup x ~24,000 deaths x ~65% wake share).
landscape.growthRate: Death-rate side ~3-4% CAGR through 2035 (ageing demographic); transparent-pricing sub-segment growing materially faster as Direct Funeral Services and 1Last expand.
landscape.wakeEmbalmedShare: ~65% (3-5 day wake with embalming and casket)
landscape.typicalCasketHiddenCostRange: S$2,000 - S$4,000 marked up inside parlour packages
landscape.passageTransparentRange: S$499 - S$1,299 all-in (casket + island-wide delivery + venue positioning + GST)
keyTrends: Anchor incumbents (Singapore Casket, Ang Chin Moh, Trinity, Nam Shan) still phone-first and quote-on-call — leaves a structural transparency gap
Direct Funeral Services and 1Last are normalising published package pricing, but still bundle casket inside the package
JB and SG manufacturers (Millennium, TSTee, Sinseeseng) sell trade-only at S$400-S$1,100, exposing the markup that retail packages hide
Eco / paper / bamboo casket category is small but growing on FSC and biodegradability narratives
Direct cremation is ~35% of SG deaths and rising, compressing the casket SKU into a bare-bones cremation-spec product
Younger SG bereaved families increasingly research online before calling, raising demand for transparent DTC casket pricing
regulatoryNotes: NEA Death Certificate / Certificate for Burial or Cremation must be issued before any final disposition; affects scheduling but not casket SKU
MOH Death Certificate process (registration with ICA) gates the timeline — Passage's 1-3 hour delivery slots into this window
ROOMS cremation slots at Mandai are scheduled by NEA / NEA-licensed crematoria — capacity constraints can force families into faster decisions, increasing pressure-tactic exposure
Workforce: Tripartite Standards (Fair Employment, Flex Work, Unpaid Care Leave), Progressive Wage Model (PWM) and TAFEP apply to all SG operators handling local staff
Supply chain: FSC Chain of Custody is the only audited timber-sustainability standard relevant to casket manufacturing; differentiator vs unmarked timber
landscape.seaContext: Casket supply chain is regional: SG demand fed by JB manufacturers (Millennium, TSTee) plus a handful of SG operators (Sinseeseng, Soon Teck, Zhen Feng). Eco-category seeded from Selangor (The Eco Casket) and Vietnam (Bamboo Eco Coffin). No SEA-wide DTC casket brand exists yet — Passage has a clean structural opening.

commonWeaknesses: Casket cost never priced as a standalone SKU (bundled and opaque)
Quote-on-call gating that increases friction for grief-stricken buyers
Brochure-ware websites with no real e-commerce checkout
No FSC / sustainability disclosure or carbon footprint reporting
No charity / governance stack tied to a binding company constitution
passageAdvantages: All-in transparent S$499 / S$999 / S$1,299 published casket pricing — only DTC casket-only brand in SG
3-hour island-wide delivery as standard, with 2hr (+S$75) and 1hr emergency (+S$150) surge options
FSC Chain-of-Custody certified, 60% locally-sourced timber, <5% material waste at assembly
30% of net profits to charity (binding via company constitution, audited and quarterly published) — FY2024 S$48,200 across 3 partners
Free-casket programme: 47 caskets given FY2024 (31 social-worker referrals + 16 direct family, 8 welfare-org partners)
Tripartite Standards triple-signatory + Progressive Wage Model + annual Scope 1+2 carbon reporting governance stack

borrowed: Titan Casket-style fully-published casket SKU pricing
Direct Funeral Services-style faith-aware bundling clarity
improved: Casket-only pricing isolated from full funeral package (none of the SG players publish this)
3-hour island-wide delivery promise that no casket retailer globally offers (vs Titan's days-long ship)
FSC + 60% local timber + <5% waste sustainability stack disclosed publicly
30% net profit charity covenant baked into company constitution and quarterly audited
Tripartite + PWM + carbon report workforce governance stack — none of the parlours publish equivalents
